circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island spans 5. 281 kilometres and was designed by Hermann Tilke to host the Formula One season finale since 2009. As the second Middle Eastern venue after Bahrain, it preceded the later additions of Qatar and Saudi Arabia to the F1 calendar.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a street track that loops around Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb and hosts the annual Australian Grand Prix as its premier Formula One event, alongside Supercars racing and other support categories. The venue holds an FIA Grade 1 classification.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit originally operated as the Österreichring and staged the Austrian Grand Prix annually throughout the 1970s and 1980s. Following a redesign and renaming, the venue returned to hosting the Grand Prix from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street track in Azerbaijan's capital, spanning 6. 003 km to rank as the fourth-longest venue in Formula One. The circuit first hosted the European Grand Prix in 2016, then became home to the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit spans 5. 412 km and hosted the inaugural Formula One race in the Middle East that same year. Beyond its annual Grand Prix, the venue hosts various motorsport categories including GP2 racing, endurance competitions, and previously accommodated Australian V8 Supercars from 2006 to 2010.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya, located near Barcelona in northeastern Spain, spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced combination of extended straights and diverse corner types that test multiple driving skills. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 status and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: Situated in the Walloon region of Belgium near the town of Spa, this 7. 004 km circuit has been hosting Formula One racing since 1950 and has become the traditional home of the Belgian Grand Prix, with continuous annual races from 1985 onwards apart from two cancelled editions. The track's lengthy layout and dramatic elevation changes across the Ardennes landscape have made it a notable venue throughout the sport's modern era.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, this 4. 361 km circuit hosts the Formula One Canadian Grand Prix annually. Beyond F1, the track has served as a venue for various international motorsport competitions, including world sportscar championships and North American racing series.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: The Shanghai International Circuit, located in Shanghai's Jiading District, hosted the Chinese Grand Prix from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. The track has also begun hosting the Shanghai ePrix racing event from 2024 onwards.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone Circuit, located in England near Northamptonshire, has served as the venue for the British Grand Prix since 1948 and notably hosted the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After rotating among three British circuits for three decades, the Grand Prix became a permanent fixture at Silverstone from 1987 onwards, which also regularly stages the MotoGP British round.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, located near Budapest in Hungary, is a 4. 381 km permanent circuit that hosted the first Formula One Grand Prix held in Eastern Europe when it debuted in 1986. Built in just eight months following Bernie Ecclestone's interest in bringing the sport to the Eastern Bloc, the facility was constructed outside the city near a highway rather than as a street circuit through Budapest's central park as originally proposed.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing facility constructed in 1922, making it mainland Europe's oldest purpose-built circuit and only the third globally after Brooklands and Indianapolis. Home to the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, the track earned the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its characteristic long straights and swift corners that enable high-velocity racing.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km Japanese motor racing facility operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture. The track hosts prestigious Formula One and Super Formula championships and accommodates up to 155,000 spectators.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, utilizing roads adjacent to the famous Las Vegas Strip. The layout threads past iconic venues such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, combining portions of Las Vegas Boulevard with surrounding city streets.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: This 4. 304 km Mexico City circuit is named after brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both accomplished drivers who died in racing accidents during the 1960s and 1970s. The venue has returned as a Formula One race destination since 2015, now operating under its current layout and officially branded as the Mexico City Grand Prix, though it previously hosted the championship on different configurations ending in 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, featuring a 3. 363-mile layout with 19 corners that produces average qualifying speeds near 139 mph. Designed by specialist circuit architects for Formula One, the venue joined the championship calendar in 2022 as the home of the Miami Grand Prix.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Monaco Grand Prix takes place on a 3. 337 km street circuit that winds through Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts, encompassing the harbour area of the principality. The track is formally and colloquially known as Monte Carlo, reflecting its location within that neighbourhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, Circuit Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km track that reclaimed its place on the Formula One calendar in 2021 with the revival of the Dutch Grand Prix. The venue will host the prestigious event until 2026, when its Formula One partnership concludes.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: The Lusail International Circuit, situated north of Doha in Qatar, is a 5. 419-kilometre motorsport venue featuring a 1. 068-kilometre main straight and holding FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km circuit in São Paulo, Brazil, originally opened in 1940 and renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his death in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The anticlockwise track hosts major international motor racing events and features an adjacent kart facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: A street circuit positioned along Jeddah's waterfront promenade overlooking the Red Sea, this venue became home to Saudi Arabia's Formula One Grand Prix in 2021 and has since expanded to accommodate Formula E racing as well.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's waterfront district, incorporating portions of the Downtown Core and Kallang planning zones into its racing layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located near Barcelona in Catalonia, this 4. 657 km circuit in Montmeló features a balanced layout combining extended straightaways with diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for Formula One teams. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: Located in Austin, Texas, the Circuit of the Americas is a 3. 426-mile Grade 1 facility that hosts the Formula One United States Grand Prix alongside major motorsport events including NASCAR, MotoGP, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race. The track has also accommodated various other racing series including IndyCar and multiple sports car championships throughout its history.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alex Albon, a Thai and British motorsport professional, currently drives for Williams in Formula One while racing under the Thai flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Fernando Alonso is a Spanish driver currently racing for Aston Martin in Formula One, having secured two world championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 Grand Prix victories. Beyond his single-seater success, he has achieved remarkable accomplishments in endurance motorsport, including the World Endurance Championship, multiple Le Mans victories, and a Daytona triumph, making him uniquely successful across both disciplines.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli made his Formula One debut in 2025 and currently races for Mercedes, having secured five Grand Prix victories in his early career.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British racing driver currently competing in Formula One as part of the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabi Bortoleto currently races in Formula One as a member of the Audi team. He has established himself as a competitor in the sport's top racing series.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas brings extensive experience to Cadillac's Formula One programme, having secured two runner-up finishes in the drivers' championship during his tenure with Mercedes. Across his 13-year career in the sport, he has accumulated 10 Grand Prix victories.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto is an Argentine racing driver competing in Formula One for Alpine, bringing South American representation to the grid.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan, an Australian driver, holds a reserve position with Haas in Formula One while competing in prototype racing for Nielsen Racing. He has made seven Grand Prix appearances between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. Previously competing for AlphaTauri, he achieved a Grand Prix victory at Monza in 2020.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar, who holds French and Algerian nationality, races in Formula One for Red Bull Racing, competing under the French flag.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver who currently races for Ferrari and stands as one of the sport's most successful competitors, having claimed seven world championships that match Michael Schumacher's record. He leads the all-time statistics across multiple categories, including the most grand prix victories, pole positions, and podium finishes.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg, a German driver, currently races in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his F1 career, he achieved endurance racing success by winning the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 while driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand-born racing driver currently competing in Formula One with the Racing Bulls team.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc represents Monaco in Formula One racing and drives for Ferrari. The 2022 season saw him finish as championship runner-up, while he has accumulated nine Grand Prix victories throughout his nine campaigns in the sport.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver competing in endurance racing for BMW with WRT and part-time in NASCAR's top series with Trackhouse Racing. The former Formula One competitor raced in the championship across two separate periods, from 2014 to 2020 and again from 2022 to 2024.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver currently racing for McLaren, claimed the Formula One World Drivers' Championship in 2025. Across his eight-season career in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The driver achieved a Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian event while competing for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Sergio Pérez, a Mexican driver, currently races for Cadillac in Formula One. The experienced competitor finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while driving for Red Bull and has accumulated six Grand Prix victories throughout his 15-season career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian driver currently racing for McLaren in Formula One, where he has secured nine Grand Prix victories over his four seasons in the championship.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo, known by his "Honey Badger" nickname, competed in Formula One for 14 seasons between 2011 and 2024, accumulating eight Grand Prix victories during his career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: George Russell is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Mercedes, having claimed seven Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-year career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz Vázquez de Castro currently races for Williams in Formula One, where he has accumulated four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-year career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver currently competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship with Proton Competition in the LMGT3 category. He previously raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to sports car racing, including a stint in IMSA's LMP2 class with Era Motorsports.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian driver currently competing in Formula One with the Aston Martin team.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Japanese driver Yuki Tsunoda participated in Formula One racing between 2021 and 2025, currently holding a reserve driver position with Red Bull Racing and its sister team Racing Bulls.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch-Belgian driver Max Verstappen races for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive world championship titles between 2021 and 2024. Over his 12-season career, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu represents China as the sole driver from his nation to have competed in Formula One, participating in the championship between 2022 and 2024. He currently holds a reserve driver position with Cadillac in the sport.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine F1 operates from Enstone and has raced under its current identity since 2021, when Renault rebranded its works team to showcase its sports car division. The squad, which powered Mercedes engines previously as Lotus, secured its only victory during this period when Esteban Ocon triumphed at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix before Renault's exit from the sport in 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British automotive manufacturer, has maintained an intermittent presence in Formula One through different team configurations, currently competing under the AMR GP Limited banner as Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's longest-surviving and most accomplished team, having participated in every championship season since 1950. The outfit is colloquially referred to as "the Prancing Horse" after its iconic emblem.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas established this American-licensed Formula One team in 2014, making its competitive debut in 2016. The squad has been led by team principal Ayao Komatsu since January 2024, succeeding Guenther Steiner who held the position from the team's founding.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Established in 1970 by Peter Sauber, this Swiss engineering firm initially built sports cars before transitioning to Formula One in 1993 as an independent constructor. The team competed for over three decades, becoming one of motorsport's longest-running organizations, until its acquisition by Audi in 2024 marked the beginning of a new chapter for the operation.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that operates as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group under the ownership of Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has been a dominant force in Formula One since 1954, operating as both a constructor and engine supplier across multiple eras. The team, currently based in Brackley and competing under the Mercedes-AMG Petronas banner with a German licence, underwent a significant ownership restructuring in 2022 when Ineos acquired an equal one-third stake alongside Mercedes-Benz and team principal Toto Wolff. The marque's single-seater efforts are traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that entered the championship in 2024, operating as the second factory team under Austrian conglomerate Red Bull's ownership alongside Red Bull Racing. Headquartered in Faenza with an additional facility near Milton Keynes, the team represents Red Bull's dual-team strategy in the sport.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that commenced competition in 2024. As one of two Red Bull-owned constructors alongside Red Bull Racing, the team operates from its Faenza headquarters with an additional facility near its sister team in Milton Keynes.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing operates as an Austrian-licensed Formula One team headquartered in England and forms part of Red Bull GmbH's two-team racing portfolio. Founded in 2005 under Christian Horner's management, the team transitioned leadership to Laurent Mekies in 2025 following Horner's departure.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One team and constructor established in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing venture. Currently competing as Atlassian Williams F1 Team, the outfit is headquartered in Grove, Oxfordshire, where it operates from a substantial purpose-built facility.
